changesProposed.name: Engine Ventures

verified.name: Engine Ventures
verified.thesis: Engine Ventures invests in Tough Tech companies commercializing breakthrough science and engineering from research labs. The firm supports early-stage deep tech startups developing hardware-based technologies across biotech, robotics, green energy, agriculture, manufacturing, material science, and quantum computing.
verified.notes: Engine Ventures spun out as a separate investment firm in 2023 from The Engine nonprofit accelerator/incubator. The Engine is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit as of 2025. Current CEO of The Engine is Emily Knight. No AUM, flagship fund name, or current senior investment partners explicitly identified in provided sources.

changes.current: Science-based companies from research labs solving massive problems. Patient capital for Tough Tech companies commercializing breakthrough science.

changesProposed.name: Future Ventures

verified.name: Future Ventures
verified.thesis: Future Ventures supports seed and early-stage investments in mission-driven entrepreneurs with ideas that have the potential to reinvent entire industries. The firm focuses on frontier technologies at the cutting edge of disruptive innovation and new industry formation.
verified.notes: Sources state firm has backed founders for 'the past 25 years' but do not specify a founding year. AUM, flagship fund name, and HQ location not found in provided sources. Sector focus cannot be verified from explicit sector list; sources only detail individual investment examples. Three partners identified on team page: Steve Jurvetson (co-founder), Maryanna Saenko (co-founder), and NICO Enriquez.

changes.current: Patient capital with 15-year fund structure. Steve Jurvetson. Early SpaceX/Tesla. Backing founders building technologies that reshape entire industries over decades.
